docs.title: Welcome to RapiD Engineering Documentation
docs.text: This documentation covers the RapiD Engineering suite of products.

docs.title: RapiD Vision
docs.text: Main vision processing application for industrial quality control and inspection.

docs.title: RapiD Vision Explorer
docs.text: Advanced data exploration and analysis tool for vision system outputs.

docs.title: RapiD Vision API
docs.text:
RESTful API and SignalR services for integration with RapiD Vision systems.

Select a product from the navigation to get started.

docs.title: Calibrating a Robot
docs.text: Documentation for calibrating a robot coming soon.

docs.title: Camera Calibration
docs.text:
To let the camera detect camera in real world units like millimeters we need to calibrate the camera. This is done by taking an object with known dimensions and place that in front of the camera.

The vision system should be configured to detect this object, the best way to do this is: - Use a Classic Vision configuration. - Go to settings and press 'Calibrate'. (do this on an empty canvas) - Now place the object with known dimensions in the Field of View of the camera - Move the 'Filter Range' slider so that only your object remains visible. - Now you can press the 'Camera Calibration' button

Fill in the known dimensions in the dialog. Then press Calibrate camera, and save the results.
